mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings and equity valuations upward over monthly horizons.
failure_condition: Rank IC for RSAFS year-over-year growth vs forward 20-day returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 4.0% signals labor market deterioration, reducing consumer income expectations and dragging equity returns lower over the next week.
failure_condition: Median 5-day forward return following trigger is not negative or positive frequency is less than 55% across all triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is rising concurrently with decelerating real GDP growth, monetary policy becomes contractionary against a weakening real economy, creating a negative joint signal for equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The increase in Rank IC from the interaction term over univariate models is less than 0.02 over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: After a recessionary trough, a strong rebound in industrial production (INDPRO) signals capacity utilization recovery and inventory restocking, both supportive of equity prices over a one-month forward window.
failure_condition: During post-recession quarters, the Rank IC of INDPRO growth vs 20-day forward returns is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) across identified recovery episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate, specifically below 2.0%, signals accommodative monetary policy that lowers the discount rate on equities, boosting forward stock market returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: In months when FEDFUNDS < 2.0%, the average 20-day forward S&P 500 return is not significantly greater than during non-trigger periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a virtuous cycle of employment-driven consumption, amplifying equity returns beyond what either signal alone would predict.
failure_condition: The joint signal from PAYEMS and RSAFS (e.g., orthogonalized interaction) does not achieve Rank IC improvement of at least 0.01 over the best univariate signal in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration of consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% year-over-year compels the Federal Reserve to tighten monetary policy, raising discount rates and reducing equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: During months with CPI > 2.5% YoY, the 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) compared to low-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: In the early phase of a recession recovery, a sharp upward revision in real GDP (GDPC1) signals that aggregate demand is rebounding, boosting corporate profits and short-term equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: In quarters identified as recession recovery (by NBER dating), the Rank IC of GDPC1 growth vs 5-day forward returns is negative or less than 0.03.
